payload.reason: Two-actor re-review converged on 3 fixes (both actors): (1) _parse_ts crashes on a tz-less ISO (parses to naive, not ValueError) -> TypeError vs aware on the gate hot path -> normalize naive->aware UTC + regression test; (2) doc-drift scope incomplete -> sweep ALL 'first/oldest non-terminal/first active' current-mechanism wording (status.py docstring+help -> generated cli-reference.md via doc check --fix; + verify.py/change.py/hook_entry.py + daemon test comments; exclude historical design doc); (3) OPEN-ITEMS GC deferral corrected (branch-detection unsound, attestation-on-main signal recorded). Scope 11->17 files. Re-planning.

Converged after 2 rounds of Claude-subagent + Codex re-review on the root-fix-only plan. Both actors confirmed: core algorithm sound, only ONE existing test functionally breaks (test_status_default_first_active — swept comprehensively), F401 correct, DRY faithful, GC-deferral clean. The 3 convergent fixes are incorporated: _parse_ts normalizes naive->aware (regression test); full doc-drift sweep (grep-verified comprehensive); OPEN-ITEMS GC deferral corrected to the attestation-on-main signal. Remaining validation is mechanical (naive test + grep-clean + doc check --fix + full pytest) via TDD.

Codex cross-review found a latent gate-hot-path crash Claude missed: _parse_ts assumes last_event_at is str, but PyYAML loads UNQUOTED ISO timestamps as datetime (Codex verified). The reducer currently QUOTES them (real state.yaml has 'quoted' → str → no live crash; full suite 1607 passed), so it's latent — but the resolver must not assume str (hand-edited/legacy/dumper-change state.yaml → datetime → dt.replace('Z',..) TypeError on the gate hot path). Fix: _parse_ts accepts datetime (normalize naive→aware) / non-str→datetime.min; add tests for yaml-loaded datetime + None. Claude code-review APPROVE otherwise (1 harmless NIT).

Both actors APPROVE. Claude code-review APPROVE (thorough: naive→aware no TypeError, pick_active_change pure, guards intact, doc-sweep complete, tests full-spec). Codex cross-review caught a latent gate-hot-path crash (PyYAML loads unquoted ISO timestamps as datetime → _parse_ts str-assumption); fixed (accepts datetime/non-str, never raises) + 2 tests; Codex re-confirmed APPROVE. Full suite 1609 passed, ruff+mypy clean, lint-imports KEPT.
